title: Architecture Decision Record
description: Structured output from Technical Architect documenting an Architecture Decision Record (ADR). Consumed by Backend Developer, Frontend Developer, Mobile Engineer, Cloudflare Engineer, Technical Lead, and Reviewer as a binding technical constraint. Supersedes earlier ADRs when architecture evolves.

adr_id.description: Unique ADR identifier. Convention: adr-NNN-short-slug or YYYY-MM-DD-slug.

title.description: Short, declarative title of the architectural decision.

feature_ticket_ref.description: feature-ticket.json ticket_id that triggered this ADR.

architecture_options_ref.description: Path or ID of architecture-options.json when options analysis preceded this ADR.

supersedes_adr.description: adr_id of the ADR this record replaces (if status=superseded).

context.description: What problem or situation prompted this decision. Include business and technical constraints.

affected_services.description: Services, repositories, or systems affected by this decision.

api_contract_refs.description: Paths or IDs of api-contract-spec.json artifacts impacted or created by this ADR.

options_considered.description: At least 2 options considered. Decision must reference one of these.

decision.description: The chosen option and the rationale for choosing it.

consequences.description: Expected outcomes, positive and negative, of adopting this decision.

neutral.description: Changes or trade-offs that are neither clearly positive nor negative.

boundary_rules.description: Explicit constraints that all implementing roles must respect (e.g. 'all LLM calls must route through the centralized service layer').

migration_plan.description: Steps required to migrate from the previous approach. Leave null if greenfield.

rollback_plan.description: How to reverse this decision if it proves incorrect.

review_date.description: Date by which this ADR should be reviewed for continued validity.

examples.title: COD Store Order Routing via Dedicated Assignment Branch
examples.context: The existing NewLogicAutoAssign command routes all orders to the warehouse courier pool. COD orders with a store_id need to be assigned to store-specific couriers to reduce delivery failure rates. Modifying the single existing branch risks breaking non-COD order assignment.

options_considered.option: Add conditional branch inside existing NewLogicAutoAssign.php
cons: Increases cyclomatic complexity of existing command
Harder to isolate and test independently

options_considered.option: Extract COD store routing into dedicated service class, called from command
pros: Future extensions (e.g. store priority rules) can be added without touching command
cons: More files to create; slightly higher initial cost

examples.decision: Option A (conditional branch) for v1 given scope and timeline constraints. Extract to service class in v2 if routing logic grows beyond 3 conditions.

positive: Fast to implement; low migration cost; no API contract changes
negative: Cyclomatic complexity of command increases by 1; test surface slightly wider
neutral: No persistent side effects; rollback is a clean revert

boundary_rules: Non-COD order path must not be modified in this implementation.
Store courier lookup must read from existing store-service; no direct DB query from orders-service.
Fallback to warehouse must log a warning with order_id and store_id.
examples.rollback_plan: Revert the commit. No migration required. Existing COD orders in-flight will continue with warehouse assignment until reassigned manually.
